refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these obligations are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need independent of how the requirements are recorded or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze the worksite mission context and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability, without specifying how these are recorded or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall record performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository to maintain history of baselines and calibration updates
Develop a validation process to ensure derived performance requirements comply with safety plans and operational policies
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical model that isolates the derivation of performance requirements from other system concerns, supporting independence and traceability.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and accessible
Risk that the logical model may become overly complex if too many variables are considered
Maintaining traceability requires disciplined documentation and version control processes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for maintaining traceability and history management to fully satisfy acceptance criteria and support auditability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and data processing components responsible for translating worksite mission context into performance requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data about the worksite and vehicle status to inform the logical model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends performance requirements and task directives derived from the logical model to other system modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external systems and remote operators, supporting data exchange necessary for mission context interpretation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the logical model is central to the overall solution; minimal diffusion since most data processing is internal.
Vehicle module focuses on physical control and actuation; containing the logical model here would cause significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation handles remote control aspects; partial relevance but containing the logical model here would diffuse logic across control and mission planning.
Safety Services focus on safety monitoring and alerts; containing the logical model here is weak and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for the logical model as it centralizes mission context interpretation and performance requirement derivation, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth within NGHE Solution if too many functions are centralized.
Interface dependencies on Telemetry and Tasking must be well-defined to avoid coupling issues.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by defining a model that translates the worksite mission context into performance requirements, directly fulfilling the need to derive performance requirements from mission context.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary, showing high cohesion (containment score 9) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2). The logical model is centralized here, minimizing diffusion risks. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting this choice.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry inputs and outputs tasking directives, enabling traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and maintenance of history for baselines/calibration updates.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the logical model to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well-justified, with defined interfaces supporting data flow and mission context interpretation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to avoid over-centralization of functions.
Ensure interface definitions with Telemetry and Tasking modules are precise to prevent coupling and maintain modularity.
Document traceability mechanisms explicitly to support verification of performance targets against worksite inputs.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with strong justification and no diffusion risks. The NGHE Solution module is well-chosen as the IM boundary with high cohesion and low diffusion, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here to avoid unnecessary complexity and maintain clear traceability and modularity. Recommended revisions focus on monitoring complexity and interface precision rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: To monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module and ensure interface definitions with Telemetry and Tasking modules remain precise to prevent coupling and maintain modularity.

assumptions: The current logical model sufficiently captures all necessary performance requirements derived from mission context.
The NGHE Solution module can handle the complexity of the logical model without compromising safety or maintainability.
open_risks: Potential over-centralization of functions within the NGHE Solution module leading to complexity growth.
Interface coupling risks if definitions with Telemetry and Tasking modules are not maintained precisely.
evidence_needed: Documentation of traceability mechanisms linking performance targets to worksite inputs.
Monitoring reports on complexity metrics within the NGHE Solution module over time.
